title: Simhachalam Temple
tagline: Simhachalam Temple — spiritual town of Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: Sri Varaha Lakshmi Narasimha temple, Simhachalam, is a Hindu temple situated on the Simhachalam Hill Range, which is 300 metres above the sea level in the city of Visakhapatnam, An
overview.description: Sri Varaha Lakshmi Narasimha temple, Simhachalam, is a Hindu temple situated on the Simhachalam Hill Range, which is 300 metres above the sea level in the city of Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh, India. It is dedicated to Lord Vishnu, who is worshipped there as Varaha Narasimha. As per the temple's legend, Vishnu manifested in this form (lion's head and human body) after saving his devotee Prahlada from a murder attempt by the latter's father Hiranyakashipu. topPlaces.name: Adavivaram
topPlaces.description: Adavivaram is a neighborhood situated on the western part of Visakhapatnam City, India. The area, which falls under the local administrative limits of Greater Visakhapatnam Municipal Corporation, is about 15 km from the Dwaraka Nagar which is city centre.

topPlaces.name: Simhachalam Hill Range
topPlaces.description: The Simhachala Hill Range, with the anglicised name Simhachalam Hills, are a towering mountain range (377 m) in the city of Visakhapatnam, in Andhra Pradesh state, southern India. It is one of the hill ranges in Andhra Pradesh and in the Eastern Ghats.

topPlaces.name: Muralinagar
topPlaces.description: Muralinagar is a neighbourhood of Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh, India. It has a residential growth of many buildings.

topPlaces.name: Pineapple Colony
topPlaces.description: Pineapple Colony is a neighborhood situated on the western part of Visakhapatnam City, India. The area, which falls under the local administrative limits of Greater Visakhapatnam Municipal Corporation, is lay down Simhachalam Hill Range .

topPlaces.name: Prahaladapuram
topPlaces.description: Prahaladapuram is a suburban area in Visakhapatnam city under Greater Visakhapatnam Municipal Corporation of Pendurthi in Andhra Pradesh, India. It is located 14 km Nomet from Visakhapatnam city center.

topPlaces.name: Madhavadhara
topPlaces.description: Madhavadhara is a residential area of the city of Visakhapatnam in Andhra Pradesh state, India.

topPlaces.name: Marripalem
topPlaces.description: Marripalem is a neighbourhood area in Visakhapatnam. It is a residential area in the city.

topPlaces.name: NAD Kotha Road
topPlaces.description: NAD Kotha Road, or NAD X Road, is one of the major junctions and commercial centers in Visakhapatnam, India. It is named for the Naval Armament Depot.

items.note: Adavivaram is a neighborhood situated on the western part of Visakhapatnam City, India. The area, which falls under the local administrative limits of Greater Visakhapatnam Municipal Corporation, is about 15 km from the Dwaraka Nagar which is city centre.

The Simhachala Hill Range, with the anglicised name Simhachalam Hills, are a towering mountain range (377 m) in the city of Visakhapatnam, in Andhra Pradesh state, southern India. It is one of the hill ranges in Andhra Pradesh and in the Eastern Ghats.
Muralinagar is a neighbourhood of Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh, India. It has a residential growth of many buildings.

Pineapple Colony is a neighborhood situated on the western part of Visakhapatnam City, India. The area, which falls under the local administrative limits of Greater Visakhapatnam Municipal Corporation, is lay down Simhachalam Hill Range .

Prahaladapuram is a suburban area in Visakhapatnam city under Greater Visakhapatnam Municipal Corporation of Pendurthi in Andhra Pradesh, India. It is located 14 km Nomet from Visakhapatnam city center.
Madhavadhara is a residential area of the city of Visakhapatnam in Andhra Pradesh state, India.
Marripalem is a neighbourhood area in Visakhapatnam. It is a residential area in the city.
NAD Kotha Road, or NAD X Road, is one of the major junctions and commercial centers in Visakhapatnam, India. It is named for the Naval Armament Depot.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Simhachalam Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Simhachalam Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Simhachalam Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Visakhapatnam Airport (6 km) and the nearest railway station is Visakhapatnam Junction (7 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Simhachalam Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Simhachalam Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Simhachalam Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Simhachalam Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Simhachalam Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Adavivaram, Simhachalam Hill Range, Muralinagar, Pineapple Colony, Prahaladapuram.
